summary:
SPIRITUAL PIONEERS AND AUTHENTIC MINISTRY: Brandon emphasizes the necessity of authentic spiritual dedication, urging individuals to become pioneering trailblazers rather than mere copycats or parrots of existing ministries. Drawing inspiration from figures like Kathryn Kuhlman, Billy Burke, John Kilpatrick, Charles Finney, and George Washington Carver, Brandon highlights the immense sacrifice, persistent prayer, and unyielding focus required to cultivate a true revival. Brandon warns against complacency in ministry, comparing plateauing spiritual efforts to sports teams that lose their edge after winning championships, such as the Kansas City Chiefs and the Golden State Warriors. Brandon stresses that true spiritual work often involves unnoticed, foundational preparation to ready the environment for a massive harvest of souls.

BOSTON EVENTS AND DOMESTIC INFRASTRUCTURE THREATS: Brandon recounts detailed visions concerning upcoming dangers and specific locations across the United States. Brandon describes a massive red cross over Boston, associating it with hospitals and an upcoming event around the time of the Boston Marathon on April 18th and 20th. Furthermore, Brandon details an ominous, onyx-black storm over the East Coast filled with lightning, alongside sightings of individuals wearing green and white shamrock jerseys, which Brandon connects to a basketball game between Boston and the New York Knicks. Expanding on domestic threats, Brandon describes a vision of men operating disguised boats on rivers, such as the Mississippi near New Orleans. These individuals, posing as fishermen, conduct reconnaissance missions to target critical infrastructure, utilizing homemade rocket launchers fashioned from PVC pipes and explosives to strike power plants, electric grids, and trains carrying highly flammable materials.

BORDER VULNERABILITIES AND GLOBAL CONFLICT: Brandon highlights severe national security risks stemming from unsecured borders, indicating that foreign threats have infiltrated the United States through underground tunnels in Arizona and across the northern border with Canada. Brandon warns that these vulnerabilities are actively exploited by cartels and terrorist sympathizers. On a global scale, Brandon discusses the ongoing conflict between Israel and Iran, characterizing it as a holy war aimed at bringing forth the Mahdi, rather than a conventional geopolitical dispute. Brandon insists that proposed ceasefires will be ineffective due to the deep-seated religious nature of the conflict. Additionally, Brandon recalls previous warnings about an impending military draft in the United States that would specifically mandate the conscription of young women alongside men.

POLITICAL LEADERSHIP AND OCCULTIC ATTACKS: Brandon details a coordinated demonic and occultic attack specifically targeting Donald Trump. Brandon describes visions of a dark, witchcraft-fueled storm over the White House, with practitioners engaging in dark rituals, drinking blood, and appearing to shapeshift into demonic, animalistic entities. Brandon urges intense, continuous prayer to protect Trump's mind, physical health, and discernment from these profound spiritual attacks and the influence of the deep state. Furthermore, Brandon extends these calls for prayer to Trump's immediate family members, including Melania, Don Jr., Eric, Barron, Tiffany, Ivanka, and Kai, as well as political figures like JD Vance and his wife Usha, urging Usha's conversion from false religion. Brandon also lists other public figures requiring prayer coverage, including Alex Jones, Elon Musk, Justin Bieber, and the Kardashians, while advocating for the passage of the Save America Act to secure future elections.

SPIRITUAL CANNIBALISM: Brandon issues a stern warning to established religious leaders regarding a phenomenon described as spiritual cannibalism. Brandon observes widespread insecurity and jealousy among older, entrenched pastors who feel threatened by the rising anointing of younger, newer ministers. Comparing this dynamic to King Saul throwing javelins at a young David, Brandon cautions these leaders against attempting to stifle or destroy emerging gifts out of fear of losing their platforms. Instead, Brandon encourages established ministers to mentor, embrace, and support the next generation. Brandon warns that playing church and offering lukewarm, spiritually dead services is no longer acceptable, declaring that a line has been drawn in the sand separating genuine believers from those merely participating in religious routines. Finally, Brandon recounts a recent answered prayer involving the interception of an armed individual attempting to attack a church in Houston, citing it as proof of the power of intercessory action.
rawData: [April 10, 2026]-[Come pray with me]-[https://youtu.be/4EEOGvIPfFM]-[Well, good evening, everybody. Thank you for joining us again tonight. We're going to go over just what the Lord showed us this last time and the heaviness of that video. I prayed over it most of the night last night, and we'll continue to pray over it today and for what the Lord has given us with our new assignments of praying for America, praying for President Trump, and continuing to lift our nation up, lifting up other nations, lifting up Canada, and so on and so on. It seems like it's a never-ending deal here. But you know, God has chosen us for such a time as this. And isn't it wonderful that we have said yes? I'm glad to be here. I don't know about y'all; I guess you are because you're here. But people wanted to know about the book that Diana was reading from last night because she didn't say. She goes, "You know, Braden, I didn't even say which book I was." No, she did at the very end. So I wanted to show you real quick. I won't make the broadcast real long tonight. I'm trying to keep it shorter because last night was almost three hours. I know you all have other things to do than just sitting here with me. But we'll just cover things real quick, and then we'll pray and we're just going to lift up the nation. This is the book. Let me hold it up here real quick. Can they see it well? That's the book that she's reading. And when she was reading it last night, this is who she was reading. And it's this man right here. Can you see that? We found it—somebody gave it to us or told us about it in an email. I don't know if it was him or whatever, but everybody that I know now is reading this book, especially prayer people. Pastor Kenneth and them are all reading it. They said they love it. I think it's great. There are other people asking about it and wanting to know. Let me have it one more time so you can see it. Is it good? Do they have a good photo of it? Yeah, so you can see it. You can order it if you feel led to get it. I don't sell books; I don't try to push books. I have people send me books from all over the place—self-published books, major book writers, all kinds of people trying to get me to promote their stuff on my channel so that they can sell their book and make money. I'm only led whenever the Lord leads us to read something. Diana looks at all of it, and when we're led by the Spirit of the Lord to mention it, it's because it's something that we believe we will use and apply to our own lives. Diana and I even told Pastor Kenneth last night, "This is something that I will want to read for the rest of my life, maybe once a month, once every quarter, whatever," and really try to refresh yourself on what you're feeding on spiritually with the Word and supplements of how to keep yourself stirred up and in that champion mindset. I've noticed—and a lot of people, I say it like this—with the gift that God has given me, Brandon doesn't control it, but I'm allowed to see a lot of things that people don't realize that I'm seeing. And I know, and I know, and I know. Let me ensure that the focus thing is set because I don't want to be disturbed here. I don't want to be a parrot. I noticed, just like whenever I study Kathryn Kuhlman a lot and being around Billy Burke and seeing his ministry and how he stands alone. He doesn't have a lot of people in his circle. He's always by himself. He's very to himself, very protective over the anointing that is on his life. He doesn't allow people really in his inner circle. And neither did Sister Kuhlman. There is a pioneer work, as I keep learning about all—I'm new to all this and I'm learning so much from the elders and stuff. It's really easy to be in the place of going, "Oh, we don't touch your glory, Lord," and you say certain things until you're in a position to touch it. You can parrot a lot of things until the glory is on you and pressure comes. Flesh always wants to be praised. Regardless of who you are, you always want that 'atta boy' or whatever. But I realized that's what Satan was wanting. His whole mission of operation, his M.O., was to be glorified. He wanted what God got. He wanted to exalt his kingdom. He wanted to be the Most High. "I will be bigger than Him; I'll do this." So, whenever you see people and they parrot things and they say they want end-time glory, they want the revival, they want these things—I watch, and the Lord allows me to see it. There are so many people that parrot and say they want something that they have no heart for at all. Fruit always displays what your root truly is. And I watch a lot of people because to have a revival like John Kilpatrick did at Pensacola back in the '90s, that takes some effort beyond anything that anybody will ever comprehend. To have a meeting every single day—I don't even know when they had off, or if they ever had off, like Sundays or whatever. Probably not Sundays, but if they had an off day, they had to have some rest or they alternated people out. But to really have revival in your church and say, "Listen, we're sold out. We're going to have church in here 24 hours a day, seven days a week. We're going to have revival up in this place," and not just have these little four-day tent meeting revivals where you go, "Wahoo, we had revival," and you're kidding yourself. That's not revival. You had a four-day meeting and you're calling it revival. When the Lord says, "I'm bringing revival to the nation; I'm going to bring a revival to these places," when I told you I saw Minnesota and he said, "I want to do it in Tennessee and I have Mobile, Alabama again, and I want to use John Kilpatrick again, and I'm going to use these generals"—well, why? Because they are sold out. They know what it takes. And the Lord has me praying for these individuals, these men, to be strong like Moses, that their blood will be good, that they will make it to the end, live long, and finish strong. But you have a lot of people that have been raised 'churched,' as I want to call them. There are a lot of churched people that say a lot and say, "Oh, we want this," but your heart never displays it by the fruit. Does that make sense? You can say a lot, but you're not acting it. You don't try to implement what you believe. You can say a lot of things and say, "We want to grow a ministry." Diana said one of the major classes I took in church growth and planting—they always teach you that the first five years of your ministry really determines the whole outcome for the rest of it. It's like Joel Osteen. Let me say it like this: Joel Osteen has the largest church in America. But as he got there, it plateaued. Where did it go any higher? What was the cutoff point with the church and how many people started to attend there? Is he still growing? Does he have multiple services every single week and it's bigger than Dr. Cho's church was in South Korea? There's a point of a climax and it stops in ministry. As I'm observing and watching other ministers, they say, "Well, we've been in ministry for 30 years, 20 years, 50 years," and all this stuff, and you see it, but their ministry is still the same. They hit the plateau of where they are and they really have not gone anywhere year after year. And you watch certain things, but they talk about the revival. They talk about this and that. As a young student, I watch and ask, "How do I implement what I'm learning?" because I'm just starting out and learning from many people. I watch big ministries all around now, and I am privy to a lot of conversations that I never was before. But I don't want to be a parrot; that's what I'm trying to say. When you are a groundbreaker into something that has never been done before—Kathryn Kuhlman was persecuted. They said she was a witch. They said she's a woman and she can't be used of God because she is a woman. "You can't use a woman." And Kathryn Kuhlman was a woman. She went through so much to get where she was—most people would never have been there—but she was sold out, and it was real. This is versus somebody who's in ministry that has always been there because their mom and dad were in ministry, so they just followed behind them and inherited what they had. There's a big difference between being like a Billy Burke—being a trailblazer that is still going strong—and hearing some people that are parrots. I watch a lot of parrots; I watch the parrots. But where is it truly at in you when you say, "I want revival"? I'm trying to challenge you right now because this is going to cost you something. What we're about to see, what we're about to participate in, is not something that we can just dip in when we feel comfortable with it. "Well, the glory's here, brother. Hallelujah!" And then all of a sudden, you're just like, "You know what? I feel like going to the movies tonight, and I don't really want to have service because it's not convenient." This isn't going to be convenient. I've never had the opportunity to talk to John Kilpatrick about it, but if I ever did, it would be a blessing. I always think when I process and watch Azusa, and they said it lasted for so many years and it went every day. William Seymour would sit in the corner and put the box on his head and do those things. What did it take for this man praying for Finney—what was his name? Nash. This is Nash. Finney praying for the revival and him going through for the Great Awakening of America. But the Lord said, "I'm going to do a Great Awakening." It's not convenient to be unnoticed. He talks about not being noticed. He talks about being the person that is never thanked for the sacrifice of prayer that he did. Everybody was always looking at Finney because he was on the stage, saying, "Oh, Finney's so great." But it took Nash praying and doing this cultivation of prayer—the blood, sweat, and tears of prayer. I'm talking about the hard work to really be sold out to prayer and not go, "Well, where's my name in lights?" because your flesh always wants to be thanked. "Well, I gave this to the ministry. I gave that. I did this. Where's my name in that? I held a hand in that." And that's what the Lord was saying: "Are you really truly dead?" When Kathryn Kuhlman said, "I'm dead. I don't want to grieve the Holy Spirit." In the things she says—I thought when I watched her at a meeting and she was doing it—there is a guy uploading these things, and I watch her. But she really was sold out, and it was never about her. She always says, "I want you to take your eyes off of Kathryn for a moment and realize that you're not here for Kathryn. You're here for Jesus." And I watch and I go, "That's not something she's parroting. This was original." But there are a lot of copycats. There are a lot of copycats in the prophetic, and they say, "Well, I heard it." And maybe they did, maybe they didn't. But you have to be a pioneer. And that's when the Lord said, "Brandon, I've called you to stand alone and do this prayer channel and do what I've called you to do." But always know it's never you; you are the glove. I was out doing my yard today, and He said, "Never forget that you're just the glove. I am coming in the glove and giving the glove life. Always remember this." I sat there and said, "Yes, Lord, You remind me a lot. How thick of a head do I have?" Obviously, if the Father keeps telling you something over and over again, you have to remind yourself. I told Diana tonight, "I don't like tattoos. If you get a tattoo, I'm not condemning it, but I just don't want to mark my body up." But I told her tonight that I got a prophetic word from Billy that was very private, and I knew it was smack-dab on because it was one of those words that hits you in the stomach. I told Diana, "I keep forgetting it. I've got to get it tattooed on my forearm so every day I can look at that word that God gave me." I know it was the Lord, and I said, "I've got to write it down on my forearm so I can remind myself and never forget what God said." Because time goes by and you become spaced out. You get these words, you've got this rhema word from God, and you're just like, "Squirrel!" You're looking at the squirrel over there and you're going, "Oh, I forgot. I remember now." Diana goes, "How quickly you forget," because Diana remembers the words a lot. Diana's very smart, and thank God for her, she always sits out there and counsels me: "This is what the Lord said. Do you not remember this?" I say, "Yeah, I do. I remember it very clearly. I just sometimes think I can change it through prayer or whatever." But it was a word that the Lord said, and it was private to us. I listen to what the Spirit of the Lord said today about the broadcast: praying for you, praying for your seed, praying for whenever somebody blesses me that I'm always diligent about blessing. There's a lady who said, "Brandon, I want a baby so bad. My husband and I..." So they sowed a seed to our ministry. "Will you please pray for us?" This was several months back, and I was diligent. Diana and I both interceded, and she came back. She said, "I just want to let you know we're pregnant. The night we sowed the seed, we actually received, and we're expecting our first baby." Diana said, "I want to show you all the people who were believing for children," because God put it on our heart to pray for you when you want a child and you haven't been able to because of whatever, from the COVID vaccine or whatever. There are so many people that have hormone issues right now that cannot conceive. You would not believe the list of people believing for children that have never been able to have one and are all of a sudden having a baby—or you're pregnant and they can't find the heartbeat—and we're interceding for you. Your vine will not cast fruit before its time. We are standing with you. But that one person who said, "I've sowed this seed believing," because they gave a sacrificial gift believing for a child... I told Diana that touched me. And when I saw the fruit, they said, "We're pregnant." I'm pregnant. Thank you for being good ground and for praying for us. I told Diana, "Those are the ones who come back to tell the Lord thank you." It's not just Brandon and Diana, but it's exciting to hear your testimony and what God did in your life. Congratulations. I believe that you'll have a healthy baby and a good, healthy pregnancy. No miscarriages, nothing; that's what I'm trying to say. It's interesting—there are so many people with good hearts, and I know the Lord wants a breakthrough in your life. I really want to say I played a part in something on the other side, whatever that may be. I don't always have to be behind a pulpit. I could just be like this, even because I have a camera. I'd pray like this regardless. Last night, we prayed and prayed; Diana and I were interceding for most of the night for Boston and the game today, and all the things the Lord showed us. We'll go over that. But maybe you can be like this man who just prayed for Finney. Never take what you do as nothing. Always give God glory for everything. You are a big part of the end-time revival behind this camera as we raise up millions of people and prayer warriors all around this world. Prayer is what's going to get the manifestation of what we're believing for. I truly believe it. I'm always challenged; I try to challenge myself with books and things to stir myself up to always keep at the forefront that it is Him, not you. I don't want to be a parrot or someone riding on someone's tailwind—like when you see someone riding in the draft of a semi. I want to be a trailblazer, a pioneer worker. There are stones all around, but we're going to make the soil ready with prayer for people who have the anointing to bring them in. It's not always going to be fun or noticed, but you may be the one picking the rocks out so the ground is soft and pliable for when the Charles Finneys of this generation or the Billy Grahams come along. If that's what God's called me to be—to dig rocks out of the ground and to pray—then we all have our part to play. Regardless of whether you are a huge speaker in a big auditorium or not, I don't want to plateau. I'm not criticizing Joel; I just know there's a plateau in every ministry unless you keep grabbing and don't become comfortable and satisfied with where you are. Pastor Chip always talks about championship teams. Did the Kansas City Chiefs become comfortable after they won so many times? Why were they so terrible last year? They had the same players, but they were really bad and weren't fun to watch anymore. You become complacent. The Golden State Warriors were good for a while with Steph Curry and were fun to watch. You had a dynasty there that fell apart. The same thing happened with Michael Jordan's team. I don't ever want to be that championship team that falls apart for the Lord. You should always be grabbing, pulling yourself up, going from glory to glory, and not being satisfied with repeating somebody else's words. I want to challenge you to keep growing and seeking Him. There's always something new you can learn from the Holy Spirit. I like George Washington Carver, the man who talked with the flowers. Sister Billie introduced that book, and she always talks about George Washington Carver. He was an African-American man during a hard time with prejudice and racism, but he prevailed and was able to succeed whenever most African-Americans weren't. He had that bulldog mentality: "I'm going to make it. I don't care what anyone else says; I'm going to do what God's called me to do." I respect George Washington Carver. I read his book, "The Man Who Talked with the flowers." My God, that guy went through something in a very racist area, but he never let the racism define him. He became what God called him to be. He would get up every morning and talk to the Father, the Great Creator. He was a trailblazer. You can't let the color of your skin, your gender, or anything else tell you you're not good enough. When God puts His hand on you, I don't care what they say; you will be what God called you to be. When I was a janitor, I asked to preach the gospel, and they said, "No, you're a janitor; go clean. I don't see the call on your life." But I felt God told me. A lot of people may not see it, but Jesus does, and He said, "I want you to go on YouTube and pray." So, even if people don't acknowledge your call or want you in their inner circle, it doesn't matter. I always look for the camaraderie of things. How many times did Brother Kenneth Hagin hang out with Oral Roberts? I've never seen them preach together, but they were in the same city, almost on the same street. Yet, I hardly ever saw them preaching at each other's conferences. They had some of the biggest ministries. I drive by them and get inspired by the mega-church, the grounds, and the school. I drive by and look at the City of Faith, the big tower, and the things spinning on the top of the building. I drive around Oral Roberts University to keep myself inspired. Even if you have the same mission, some camps don't function with other camps. I'm learning because I'm in one of the greatest cities in the Bible Belt. And maybe I'm wrong. Maybe they hung out every day; I just never saw it on video because I watch all of them. But I hardly ever hear them talking about each other very often, even in their sermons. But yet they were that close together. You know what I'm saying? I mean, they're like less than five miles apart probably. And I'm thinking, because I drive over there, I'm looking and they're almost on the same street. But does that make them bad? No. It just means they did what they were called to do. And there's a whole lot of other ministries here. There's all kinds of churches in Tulsa. I don't know. I'm just learning, and I watch and I say, 'Hmm, this is all so interesting to me.' So people go, 'Well, boy, you treading on really soft, rice paper thin. You shouldn't say some of the things you say.' I ain't scared of it. I just look at it and I'm just real. I'm not trying to make something out of nothing, but I just watch. And like I said, maybe that's why I'm where I'm at and they are where they are at. I always ask the hard questions because I want to learn, because I want to go and be close to Him. Y'all have an opinion and it changes all the time. The same person who gets excited and tells me I'm good in one email is mad the next couple of days, telling me I'm going to hell. And I get them all the time. 'We really like you.' And then the next one, you're mad and you're saying profanity at me in emails. And I'm going, 'That's interesting.' D goes, 'Isn't that interesting?' I said, 'No, it's just people.' All right. So, be a trailblazer. Be somebody who is getting the rocks out of there like us. And when it's all said and done, no matter who's in your tent and who's in your camp, we're in a time like we've never seen before. And we're going to touch the nations for Jesus. And we may be the only ones in our little group—me, my son, and my wife—and we're all standing together. We don't have to have people in our corner being our cheerleader and telling us how great we are or what we're doing. Jesus already did that: 'I love you, Brandon. Keep going.' And we just keep doing what He's asked us to do. And we're so excited that there are so many of you that show up and want to pray with us. But that's what I had to say tonight. Every single night, I always try to be a little different for things that are on my heart and things that I'm thinking. You say, 'That's Brandon's thoughts today.' All right. There. Now, tell them what you want to tell them because I told them what I felt like I wanted to say. You're done. What was it that happened? Yeah. I can go over some of the things we saw last night. We saw a lot. And I'm going to tell you this: after I saw what I saw, it shook me. And there's a lot of you that think that we're years and years away from Jesus Christ coming back, and you are greatly deceived. Jesus is coming, and He's coming very soon. And as I was looking into the Boston Marathon and things like that, praying over the 20th and the 18th—the Lord highlighted the 20th and the 18th—and Coachella, praying over these oil wells. There was so much that the Lord had us praying for last night: praying over the NBA, praying over the NBA teams, certain ones. And like I said, I don't say a whole lot because there's a lot of you that gamble, and you go, 'Well, you're telling me to...' It's not a gamble. I pray over this and they start accusing me: 'Are you saying this team? Are you saying this team and this team are going to be together in the playoffs?' I'm telling you what I'm praying over. So then you want to go and gamble, and it's like, my God, what is wrong with the immaturity of some of these people? I'm not saying that. The Lord said to pray over these teams. You can pray over Boston. You can pray over New York. You can pray over these teams. Both look like they could be doing something in the future. I don't know. I don't follow sports a whole lot. The Lord just talks about sports because He knows I like the Thunder. And so, okay, this is very serious. Do you remember when I told you I saw a draft coming? Do you remember when I told you that they would use women in the draft? And I said I saw Trump being president during this time frame. I said, 'I saw a draft coming to America, and they didn't just draft the men; they drafted the women.' And I said, 'Pray against this. Pray against it.' Does anybody remember that? Put it in the comments section right now if you remember me talking about the draft and that they would draft your women too because they wanted to have equal rights. And I said women would be drafted too, and they would be called into service—a younger generation of women. Does anybody remember that? I saw it in the vision and we were praying over it, and I said they were going to Europe and they were going over places because we got struck on America's soil. I don't know if you all remember this; the Lord shows us quite a bit and we're grateful for everything. But now I want you to see what we prayed over a year and a half or two years ago is now in the news. Can you show them that now? That disturbed me a little bit. Is there more? So, you see they're talking about automatically doing all this and making sure your kids are ready for war. Now, I don't agree with that. I don't agree with any war, but especially forcing people to go fight if they didn't register for it and they don't want to do it. But it's sad because I told you I saw it coming, what the Lord showed us about a major draft and that they would have to do it in the days ahead. And so many women got so upset about it and freaked out about it. Now, I'm trying to tell you what the Lord showed me. And I'm not trying to stir up fear. I'm trying to tell you we must pray because of even what I told you last night. Okay, this is what I thought was interesting. Thank you, Holy Spirit. He starts helping me because I wait for the anointing to hit me, and then finally I start feeling it come on me. So, y'all have to bear with me because I can't do this without Him. But I saw a red cross. The Red Cross was over Boston and the Lord said, 'Pray over this time frame, for it will be about the Red Cross.' And I told you that. Does anyone remember that last night? And I said, 'I see a red cross. It's about hospitals. I see hospitals with red crosses on them.' See, I hear in part and I see in part, but a lot of times I don't know what I'm seeing. I just call out the vision of what I'm seeing as I'm praying. And I said, 'It's about a red cross.' And I said, 'There's a massive red cross, so much so that a helicopter could land on it.' Does anybody remember that? I said, 'I see it, and it looks like it's about hospitals.' So, not wanting to add to or take away from anything, the Red Cross has a Run for the Red Cross in Boston on the 18th. Pretty interesting, huh? It's a Red Cross. And I said, 'I see red crosses everywhere. It looks like hospitals.' Just remember that, because I don't know what I'm saying. But they take notes. Braden—he's over there taking notes. He has everything down and he's taking notes for everything the Lord's showing because he knows how the gift flows and he knows what we're looking for afterwards, because I can't stop and say, 'Write this down.' Braden's trained and he knows exactly what we are looking for. Okay. So, the Red Cross runner, the Red Cross... see that? It's on April the 20th, right? So, it's during this time frame, but the Boston Marathon starts all next week and it goes for several days with several different kinds of events, from what I understand. But it's a red cross. So, isn't that kind of interesting how the Lord does that? I think it's interesting because I thought, 'Why am I seeing all these hospitals?' The Lord said, 'It's not about that. It's about the Red Cross. It's about this hospital.' And I thought, 'What are you showing me?' I said, 'The hospitals are in trouble,' because that's what I'm seeing. Hospitals are in trouble. What is it about this Red Cross? So then I saw a massive storm over Boston and I said it's over the New York area, all up there in the East. But I said this storm is so black, like onyx, that you can't see through it. And there are so many lightning bolts coming out of it. It looks like an electric storm. In this storm, it is so massive and so dark that it's coming, and I could see it. Then I saw people with shamrocks and green and white outfits and jerseys walking down the street. I said, 'I see these people celebrating basketball.' I said, 'Then I see the Knicks, the New York Knicks.' And I said, 'Watch these two teams.' What does that mean? I don't know. They're going to play each other. People said, 'Brandon, excuse me.' People said, 'Brandon, they're playing tonight,' which was tonight. Well, nothing happened. The game's over, thank God.
